title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
summary: Three architectural patterns for long-running AI agents are presented: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable production-grade agents capable of managing multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbots.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video presents three architectural patterns for building long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. The patterns target agents that manage multi-day workflows, moving past the limits of stateless chatbots toward production-grade systems. Event-driven dormancy lets agents idle until triggered, checkpointing preserves state durably across long runs, and separated evaluation decouples assessment from execution. Subagents are also covered as part of the design.
